topic.name: Tariff Schedule Amendments
topic.description: Documents related to temporary suspensions of duties on specific materials and products in the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States.

Document 1: suspend temporarily the duty on acrylic resin solution
Document 2: suspend temporarily the duty on certain injection-molded ABS or PP cases or containers
Document 3: extend the temporary suspension of duty on magnesium peroxide

topic.name: Housing Finance Reform
topic.description: Legislation aimed at restructuring housing finance mechanisms, including mortgage-backed securities and home equity programs.

Document 1: Amends the National Housing Act to remove the Government National Mortgage Association (Ginnie Mae) from the Department of Housing and Urban Development (HUD) and establish it as an independent entity.
Requires Ginnie Mae to establish a method of assessing a national average single-family house price for use in calculating the loan limits for single-family mortgage loans.
Document 2: Amends the National Housing Act to require that, to be eligible for insurance under the Home Equity Conversion Mortgage program for elderly homeowners, a mortgage contain terms and provisions with respect to property maintenance.
Authorizes the Secretary of Housing and Urban Development (HUD) to establish any additional or alternative requirements necessary to more effectively carry out such Act.

topic.name: Surveillance and Privacy Legislation
topic.description: Legislation aimed at regulating government surveillance practices and protecting individual privacy rights.

Document 1: Amends the Foreign Intelligence Surveillance Act of 1978 (FISA) to prohibit federal officers from searching for a particular U.S. person in communications acquired under authorization.
Prohibits a federal agency from requiring or requesting manufacturers to design products for surveillance.
Document 2: Requires any agency or business entity with sensitive personally identifiable information to notify without unreasonable delay any U.S. resident of a security breach.
Exempts agencies from notification requirements if it would impede a criminal investigation or damage national security.
Document 3: Permits electronic communications providers to report information about government requests for information under surveillance laws.
Defines 'surveillance law' to include the Foreign Intelligence Surveillance Act of 1978 and other specified acts.

topic.name: School Health and Leadership Initiatives
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative initiatives aimed at improving school leadership and health-related support systems within educational institutions.

Document 1: establish a school leadership academy that develops and provides evidence-based leadership training to school leaders
transform and improve turnaround schools
Document 2: direct the Secretary of Education to award matching grants to local educational agencies to purchase automated external defibrillators
provide training in cardiopulmonary resuscitation (CPR)
Document 3: authorize the Secretary of Education to award grants to schools that are receiving school improvement funds
develop and implement an asthma management plan

topic.name: Tax Code Amendments for Benefits
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative changes to the Internal Revenue Code aimed at enhancing tax benefits for transportation, veterans' travel, and education savings.

Document 1: equalize and increase to $200, the tax exclusion for both transportation and parking fringe benefits
Extends such benefits to federal employees.
Document 2: allow veterans a deduction from gross income for their travel expenses
related to a service-connected disability
Document 3: increase from $2,000 to $4,000 the annual contribution limit to Coverdell education savings accounts
Makes permanent provisions enacted by the Economic Growth and Tax Relief Reconciliation Act of 2001

topic.name: Environmental Protection and Conservation
topic.description: Legislation focused on the protection and sustainable management of natural resources and ecosystems.

Document 1: Establishes in the Department of Energy (DOE) a research, development, and commercial application program to assure the long-term, secure, and sustainable supply of energy critical elements
encourage multidisciplinary collaborations and opportunities for students at institutions of higher education
Document 2: authorize the Secretary of the Interior to award a multiyear grant to an eligible person to implement a great ape conservation project
Requires the Secretary to convene a panel of experts to identify the greatest needs and priorities for the conservation of great apes
Document 3: requires the Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) to develop and publish within 180 days a health advisory with recommendations on: the level of microcystins in drinking water that is safe for human consumption
the EPA must report annually on the status of the EPA's efforts to determine whether to regulate microcystins in drinking water

topic.name: Renewable Energy Tax Legislation
topic.description: Legislation focused on tax incentives and regulations for renewable energy investments and technologies.

Document 1: Amends the Internal Revenue Code, with respect to tax-exempt new clean renewable energy bonds
allow such unlimited bonds to be issued until January 1, 2014
Document 2: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to repeal tax credits for alternative motor vehicles
Directs the Secretary of the Treasury to revise the income tax rates for corporations based upon the overall revenue savings from the repeal of the energy tax expenditures
Document 3: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to extend through 2018 the tax credit for producing electricity from certain renewable resources
Includes marine and hydrokinetic renewable energy as a renewable resource for purposes of such credit

topic.name: Child Welfare and Law Enforcement Reform
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative efforts aimed at improving child welfare, preventing abuse, and reforming law enforcement practices to ensure accountability and protect vulnerable populations.

Document 1: promote the well-being of maltreated infants and toddlers
prevent the recurrence of child abuse and neglect
Document 2: analyze existing law enforcement accreditation standards
establish the Task Force on Law Enforcement Oversight
Document 3: provide state, local, or tribal law enforcement agencies with technical assistance in the investigation or prosecution of violent crimes and hate crimes
expand data collection and reporting requirements under such Act

topic.name: Veterans Affairs Legislation
topic.description: Legislation related to the rights and services provided to veterans, including identity theft protections and mental health services.

Document 1: Identity Theft Improvement Act of 2010
eliminate the requirement in a prosecution for identity theft
Document 2: Secretary of Veterans Affairs to furnish
grave of any veteran who died before November 1, 1990
Document 3: Secretary of Veterans Affairs to contract
provision of veterans' mental health services

topic.name: Workplace Accommodations and Leave
topic.description: The documents focus on legal provisions related to workplace accommodations for health-related conditions and leave entitlements for employees.

Document 1: unlawful employment practice for employers to fail to make reasonable accommodations to known limitations related to pregnancy
requires such employees to take leave if another reasonable accommodation can be provided
Document 2: prohibit courts from approving or ordering the enforcement of any provision of a settlement agreement in a civil suit if it relates to public safety or health
Document 3: amends the Family and Medical Leave Act to provide for employee leave to care for a same-sex spouse or other family members with serious health conditions

topic.name: Telecommunications Safety Legislation
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation aimed at enhancing safety and regulatory standards in telecommunications, particularly regarding emergency services and child protection from inappropriate content.

Document 1: This bill amends the Communications Act of 1934 to prohibit businesses from manufacturing or importing for use in the United States, or selling or leasing in the United States, a multi-line telephone system unless it is pre-configured to allow users to directly initiate a call to 9-1-1.
Businesses are prohibited from installing, managing, or operating multi-line telephone systems without such a direct 9-1-1 call configuration.
Document 2: Amends the Public Health Service Act to revise sanctions for laboratories that intentionally refer proficiency testing samples required for certification to another laboratory for analysis.
giving the Secretary of Health and Human Services (HHS) discretion to substitute intermediate sanctions for such violations.
Document 3: Requires the Federal Communications Commission (FCC) to initiate a notice of inquiry to consider measures to examine the existence and availability of advanced blocking technologies.
Defines 'advanced blocking technologies' as technologies that can improve or enhance the ability of a parent to protect his or her child from any indecent or objectionable video or audio programming.

topic.name: Public Land Management and Protection
topic.description: Legislation focused on the designation, protection, and management of public lands in the United States.

Document 1: Designates specified lands in Utah as components of the National Wilderness Preservation System
Sets forth administrative provisions regarding federal reservation of water rights
Document 2: Withdraws the approximately 1,068,908 acres of the Tusayan Ranger District in Arizona
Subjects such withdrawal to valid, existing rights
Document 3: Directs the Department of the Interior to convey to the municipality of Anchorage
Requires the municipality to pay the appraised value of the federal reversionary interest

topic.name: Cybersecurity Legislation
topic.description: This topic encompasses various legislative measures aimed at enhancing cybersecurity through information sharing, threat intelligence, and federal oversight of information security practices.

Document 1: Strengthening and Enhancing Cybersecurity by Using Research, Education, Information, and Technology Act of 2012 or SECURE IT
Allows private entities, nonfederal government agencies, or state, tribal, or local governments to voluntarily disclose cyber threat information
Document 2: Federal Information Security Modernization Act of 2014 - Amends the Federal Information Security Management Act of 2002
Requires the Secretary to develop and oversee implementation of operational directives requiring agencies to implement the Director's standards
Document 3: Cyber Intelligence Sharing and Protection Act - Amends the National Security Act of 1947
Defines 'cyber threat intelligence' as information in the possession of an element of the intelligence community directly pertaining to a vulnerability of, or threat to, a system or network

topic.name: Water Rights and Management
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation related to water rights allocation, irrigation infrastructure improvement, and the management of water resources for Native American tribes.

Document 1: conduct a study of irrigation infrastructure for 18 Rio Grande Pueblos
increase water use efficiency and agricultural productivity for the benefit of a Pueblo
Document 2: control harmful nonnative species
assess the needs to restore, manage, or enhance native fish, wildlife, and habitats
Document 3: allocate to the Tribe 50,000 acre-feet per year of stored water
rehabilitation and improvement of the Blackfeet Irrigation Project

topic.name: Transportation and Energy Policy
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation aimed at improving transportation alternatives, public safety communications, and regulations affecting energy resources and environmental impacts.

Document 1: supports community efforts to invest in transportation alternatives
requires the Secretary of Housing and Urban Development to incorporate transportation costs into affordability standards
Document 2: increases the allocation of electromagnetic spectrum for use by public safety entities
establish a nationwide, interoperable public safety broadband network
Document 3: prohibits the Secretary of the Interior from issuing regulations that would adversely impact employment in coal mines
amends the Clean Air Act to prohibit regulation concerning greenhouse gas emissions

topic.name: U.S. Trade and Economic Policy
topic.description: The documents discuss amendments related to U.S. trade policies, including tariff suspensions and cost-sharing requirements for manufacturing programs.

Document 1: Amends the National Institute of Standards and Technology Act to prohibit the Secretary of Commerce...
recommendation for structuring the cost share requirement after FY2013
Document 2: Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States to suspend temporarily the duty on certain ice cream makers.
Document 3: Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States to suspend temporarily the duty on certain tamper resistant ground fault circuit interrupters.

topic.name: Social Security and Seniors Legislation
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative measures aimed at improving financial and mental health support for seniors under the Social Security Act.

Document 1: Amends title XVIII (Medicare) of the Social Security Act
gradual reduction of copayment rates for outpatient psychiatric services
Document 2: Amends title II (Old Age, Survivors and Disability Insurance)
prohibit the receipts and disbursements of the Federal Old-Age and Survivors Insurance Trust Fund
Document 3: disburse a cash payment to recipients of Social Security
Defines a non-COLA year for benefit adjustments

topic.name: Workforce Development Initiatives
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation aimed at improving job training, local food sourcing in schools, and outreach services for veterans, all focusing on enhancing community and workforce development.

Document 1: requires the Department of Labor to award grants to employers to partner with community colleges to create programs for training individuals for new jobs
results in a net increase in employment for the employer.
Document 2: directs the Secretary of Agriculture to provide grants to states for use in providing school food authorities with local food credits
requires school food authorities to use their credits to purchase locally or regionally produced agricultural food products to serve in the school lunch program.
Document 3: Directs the Secretary of Veterans Affairs to establish, maintain, and modify as necessary procedures for ensuring the effective coordination of outreach activities
Authorizes the Secretary to make grants to state or county veterans agencies for state and local outreach services.

topic.name: Cybersecurity and Education Policy
topic.description: The documents focus on legislation related to cybersecurity measures and educational initiatives aimed at promoting access to education, particularly for women.

Document 1: Defines 'cybersecurity' as 'information security' which is defined as protecting information and information systems from unauthorized access, use, disclosure, disruption, modification, or destruction.
Requires the President to initiate a rulemaking to establish a procedure for the designation, as a critical infrastructure information system under this Act.
Document 2: Expresses the sense of Congress that every individual, regardless of gender, should have the opportunity to pursue an education free from the fear of discrimination.
Directs the USAID Administrator to increase the number of scholarships to women under the Merit and Needs-Based Scholarship Program.

topic.name: U.S. Energy Infrastructure Legislation
topic.description: Legislation focused on the construction, operation, and regulation of energy pipelines and extraction activities in the United States.

Document 1: Directs the President...to grant a permit...for the Keystone XL pipeline project

Requires the President...to report to certain congressional committees...a justification for it, including consideration of economic, employment, energy security, foreign policy, trade, and environmental factors.
Document 2: Authorizes TransCanada Keystone Pipeline, L.P. to construct, connect, operate, and maintain pipeline facilities...
Deems a certain EIS issued by the Department of State to satisfy all requirements of the National Environmental Policy Act of 1969 (NEPA).
Document 3: Amends the Outer Continental Shelf Lands Act to authorize the governor of Virginia to petition the Secretary of the Interior for authorization to conduct natural gas or crude oil exploration and extraction activities
Requires deposit of the other 50% into a special account in the Treasury from which the Secretary shall disburse...

topic.name: Animal and Food Regulation
topic.description: Legislation aimed at regulating the safety and welfare of animals and food products in commerce.

Document 1: Amends the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act to deem a food to be misbranded if it contains genetically-engineered fish unless the food bears a label stating that fact.
Document 2: Amends the Animal Welfare Act to define a 'high volume retail breeder' and requires dealers to include on licensing applications and annual renewals the total number of dogs exempted from exercise.

topic.name: U.S. Immigration and Foreign Policy
topic.description: The documents discuss aspects of U.S. immigration policy and foreign relations, particularly concerning citizenship, aid conditions, and designating organizations as terrorist groups.

Document 1: Directs the Secretary of Homeland Security to make funds available annually to support public ceremonies for administering oaths of allegiance to naturalizing legal immigrants.
Ceremonies shall be held on or near Independence Day.
Document 2: Prohibits assistance from being provided to Pakistan unless the Secretary of State certifies to Congress that Pakistan is not aiding the Haqqani network.
Makes withheld funds available for assistance to fight drug cartels on the U.S.-Mexico border.
Document 3: Expresses the sense of Congress that the Muslim Brotherhood has met the criteria for designation as a foreign terrorist organization.
Requires the State Department to report to Congress within 60 days whether the Muslim Brotherhood meets the criteria for foreign terrorist designation.

topic.name: Higher Education Financial Aid
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation related to student financial assistance, including loan consolidation, debt relief, and safety regulations in educational institutions.

Document 1: Amends the Higher Education Act of 1965 to extend the Pell Grant program
Directs the Secretary to forgive the balance due on DLs and direct consolidation loans by individuals who have been public sector employees for 10 years
Document 2: This bill amends title IV (Student Assistance) of the Higher Education Act of 1965
requires an IHE that maintains on-campus student housing facilities to notify students about whether a housing facility is equipped with a fire safety system
Document 3: Amends title IV (Student Assistance) of the Higher Education Act of 1965 to allow certain borrowers to consolidate their private education loans
Directs the Secretary of Education to inform and educate students and their families about this loan consolidation program

topic.name: Environmental Management and Development
topic.description: This topic encompasses regional development efforts and species management regulations within specific geographic areas.

1: Tennessee counties of Hickman, Perry, and Wayne
2: Pacific Northwest Gray Wolf Management Act of 2015
exclusive jurisdiction over the management of wolves
3: Revises the boundaries of the Sachuest Point Unit
John H. Chafee Coastal Barrier Resources System in Rhode Island

topic.name: Environmental Regulation and Protection
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation aimed at regulating pollutants and protecting water quality, particularly through amendments to the Clean Water Act.

Document 1: Amends the Federal Water Pollution Control Act to require the EPA to issue a rule to prohibit any member of a board from seeking to influence permit approvals in which they have a financial interest.
Ensures boards exclude members with significant income from permit holders or applicants.
Document 2: Amends the Federal Water Pollution Control Act to provide that no permit shall be required for certain discharges from recreational vessels.
Directs the Administrator to develop management practices to mitigate adverse impacts on U.S. waters.

topic.name: Immigration and Employment Policies
topic.description: The documents discuss various policies and regulations regarding the adjustment of immigration status, employment verification, and visitor admission to the United States.

Document 1: Directs the Secretary of Homeland Security (DHS) to adjust the status of an eligible alien entrepreneur, alien spouse, or alien child to that of an alien lawfully admitted for permanent residency
Authorizes the Secretary to waive grounds of inadmissibility for humanitarian, family unity, or public interest purposes.
Document 2: Amends the Immigration and Nationality Act to direct the Secretary of Homeland Security (DHS) to establish an employment eligibility verification system (EEVS)
Requires an employer to attest, during the verification period and under penalty of perjury, that the employer has verified that an individual is not an unauthorized alien.

topic.name: Economic Relief Legislation
topic.description: Legislation aimed at providing financial assistance and reforms related to unemployment benefits and pension plans in response to economic challenges.

Document 1: Unemployment Compensation Extension Act of 2008
extends the period of emergency unemployment compensation.
Document 2: Amends the Employee Retirement Income Security Act of 1974
authorize the Pension Benefit Guaranty Corporation to allow the sponsor of an underfunded multiemployer benefit plan to accept...
Document 3: allows a current or former employee of a commercial passenger airline... to make a tax-free rollover of such amount to a traditional individual retirement account (IRA)
Excludes from the gross income of an airline employee amounts transferred to a traditional IRA under this Act.

topic.name: U.S. Policy on Syria and Iran
topic.description: Legislative measures addressing U.S. foreign policy, humanitarian support, and sanctions related to Syria and Iran.

Document 1: Directs the President to appoint a Special Envoy for Syria to coordinate U.S. engagement with the country
Urges the President to support humanitarian activities taking place in and outside Syria
Document 2: require the President to block and prohibit all transactions in property and property interests of a foreign person that knowingly supports or conducts certain transactions with Iran's Revolutionary Guard Corps
The President shall review an identified foreign person to determine whether there is sufficient evidence to impose sanctions
Document 3: Sets forth congressional findings regarding Iranian support for terrorism

Directs the President, with respect to sanctions against Iran, to reduce the dollar threshold for imposition of sanctions against persons making investments that contribute to Iran's petroleum resource development

topic.name: Veteran and Volunteer Support Legislation
topic.description: Legislation aimed at improving support and services for military personnel, veterans, and Peace Corps volunteers, focusing on reintegration, medical care, and victim advocacy.

Document 1: requires that a member of a reserve component of the Armed Forces who was deployed for more than 269 days be retained on active duty for 45 days after demobilization
directs the Secretary of the military department concerned to provide each member so retained reintegration counseling and services.
Document 2: requires the Secretary of Veterans Affairs and the Secretary of Defense to operate a program that ensures treatment from private providers for military sexual trauma
assign a voucher that such individual may use to receive such treatment.
Document 3: increases the monthly pay rate for Peace Corps volunteers
expands membership of the Sexual Assault Advisory Council and requires at least one member to have mental health field expertise.

topic.name: Health Care Legislation and Policy
topic.description: This cluster focuses on U.S. legislation related to health care programs, including Medicaid, kidney disease management, and electronic health records for veterans.

Document 1: Protecting the Medicaid Safety Net Act of 2008

extends until April 1, 2009, the moratorium on implementation of a proposed rule relating to the federal-state financial partnerships under titles XIX (Medicaid) and XXI (State Children's Health Insurance Program)
Document 2: Kidney Care Quality and Education Act of 2007
amends title XVIII (Medicare) of the Social Security Act to provide for Medicare coverage of kidney disease patient education services
Document 3: 21st Century Health Care for Heroes Act
directs the Secretaries of Defense and Veterans Affairs to implement electronic health record systems that provide for the full interoperability of personal health care information

topic.name: Federal Budget and Resource Allocation
topic.description: This topic encompasses regulations and directives related to the allocation and limitation of federal funds and resources across various programs and departments.

Document 1: Prohibits funds from being used to carry out the Ambassador's Fund for Cultural Preservation
grants for cultural preservation outside the United States.
Document 2: Requires the Director of the Office of Management and Budget (OMB) to issue a directive limiting agency expenditures for advertising
not more than 50% of the amount of such expenditures during FY2012.
Document 3: Directs the Secretary of Veterans Affairs to convey to the state of Texas the Thomas T. Conally Department of Veterans Affairs Medical Center
the state of Texas the Thomas T. Conally Department of Veterans Affairs Medical Center in Marlin, Texas.

topic.name: Identity Theft and Fraud Prevention
topic.description: Legislative measures aimed at preventing and addressing identity theft and fraud in various contexts.

Document 1: amends the Internal Revenue Code to require the Department of the Treasury and the Internal Revenue Service to take several actions to prevent and respond to tax-related identity theft and tax fraud

the IRS must submit to Congress a feasibility study regarding a program under which a person who has filed an identity theft affidavit with the IRS may elect to prevent the processing of any federal tax return
Document 2: requires the PHA or other reviewing body, during such procedures, to consider all mitigating circumstances and the impact of such actions upon the family and dependents of that person
authorizes a PHA or owner to deny criminal offenders admission to such programs or housing
Document 3: defines sensitive personally identifiable information
increases penalties for fraud and related activity, and imposes criminal penalties for attempts and conspiracies to commit fraud and related activity, in connection with computers

topic.name: Advanced Vehicle and Energy Technologies
topic.description: This topic encompasses the research, development, and implementation of innovative technologies aimed at reducing petroleum use and emissions in transportation and energy sectors.

Document 1: Directs the Secretary of Energy (DOE) to conduct a program of basic and applied research, development, demonstration, and commercial application activities on materials, technologies, and processes with the potential to substantially reduce or eliminate petroleum use by, and emissions from, the nation's passenger and commercial vehicles.
Requires the program to include activities in the areas of hybridization or full electrification of vehicle systems, batteries, ultracapacitors, and other energy storage devices.
Document 2: Requires the Director of the National Institute of Standards and Technology to establish a program of research, development, and demonstration, including the establishment of best practices, adaptation of existing technology, and efforts to accelerate the development of next generation technology and tracking systems for mine communications.
Authorizes the Director to focus on communications and tracking system characteristics that are likely to work in emergency situations.
Document 3: Instructs the Secretary of Energy to seek to ensure the availability of 200% of the volume of renewable fuels required to be available in the United States by 2013 under the Energy Policy Act of 2005.

Directs the Secretary to establish a loan guarantee program for up to 80% of the cost of a project for the harvesting, storing, and delivery of agriculture residues for use in cellulosic or traditional ethanol production plants.

topic.name: Drug Regulation and Safety
topic.description: Legislation aimed at improving the regulation, safety, and market access of pharmaceutical drugs.

Document 1: Amends the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act (FFDCA) to revise the definition of 'first applicant' for purposes of the 180-day exclusivity period given to first applicants to file an abbreviated new drug application (generic drug).

Prohibits a party that enters an agreement to delay seeking approval of its generic drug application or to delay the commercial marketing of a generic drug from seeking approval of its application or beginning commercial marketing.
Document 2: Amends the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act (FFDCA) to establish a criminal fine and/or imprisonment for a person who knowingly causes a prescription drug to be adulterated, misbranded, or misrepresented.
Requires HHS to educate the public and health care professionals on counterfeit drugs.

topic.name: Health Care Reform Legislation
topic.description: This cluster discusses various legislative acts aimed at reforming health care policies, particularly focusing on the repeal of the Affordable Care Act and the introduction of new tax provisions and insurance regulations.

Document 1: Repeals the Patient Protection and Affordable Care Act (PPACA) and the Health Care and Education Reconciliation Act of 2010
Amends the Internal Revenue Code to revise the refundable tax credit for the health insurance costs of individual taxpayers
Document 2: Provides that for purposes of medical professional liability insurance or civil and criminal malpractice liability determinations
deemed to have provided such medical services in the primary state
Document 3: Repeals the Patient Protection and Affordable Care Act and the health care provisions of the Health Care and Education and Reconciliation Act of 2010
Amends the Internal Revenue Code to allow a tax credit for qualified health insurance costs

topic.name: Federal Benefits and Procurement Reform
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative measures aimed at reforming federal benefits for postal and veterans' services, as well as improving federal procurement practices.

Document 1: Postal Service Health Benefits Program within the Federal Employees Health Benefits Program
annual prefunding payments to the Postal Service Retiree Health Benefits Fund must be recomputed
Document 2: amend the Federal Acquisition Regulation (FAR) to revise certain federal acquisition practices
requiring executive agency heads to use online reverse auction in the procurement of commercial items
Document 3: Department of Veterans Affairs (VA) to pay burial and funeral expenses
Board of Veterans' Appeals shall determine whether to provide such hearing

topic.name: Tariff Suspension Legislation
topic.description: Legislation related to the temporary suspension of duties on specific goods in the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States.

Document 1: suspend temporarily the duty on economizers
use with boilers of heading 8402 or 8403
Document 2: suspend temporarily the duty on certain footwear
for other purposes
Document 3: extend the temporary suspension of duty
on Paraquat dichloride

topic.name: Public Health Legislation
topic.description: Legislation aimed at enhancing public health response, research, and support for various health conditions and emergencies.

Document 1: establishes and provides funds for the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention (CDC) Emergency Response Fund
use this fund when emergency operations are activated in response to a public health emergency
Document 2: authorizes appropriations for conducting and supporting research on Alzheimer's disease
requires the Director of the National Institute on Aging to give priority to conducting and supporting Alzheimer's disease research
Document 3: direct the Secretary of Health and Human Services (HHS Secretary) to establish a demonstration program
develop, demonstrate, and disseminate a standard curriculum for training first responders in assisting individuals with autism

topic.name: Indian Land Rights and Management
topic.description: Legislation and policies governing the management, transfer, and use of land held in trust for federally recognized Indian tribes.

Document 1: Requires the Secretary of the Interior to transfer land the federal government holds in trust for a federally recognized Indian tribe to such tribe as restricted fee tribal land.
Gives the laws of a federally recognized Indian tribe that establish a system of land tenure precedence over any federal law or regulation.
Document 2: Declares that the United States holds approximately 373 acres of BLM administered land in trust for the Te-moak Tribe of Western Shoshone Indians of Nevada.
Makes such land part of the Tribe's reservation.
Document 3: Amends the San Luis Rey Indian Water Rights Settlement Act to direct the Secretary of the Interior and the Attorney General to take all necessary or appropriate steps to execute and implement the settlement agreement.

topic.name: Legislative Procedures and Reforms
topic.description: This topic encompasses various legislative acts aimed at reforming congressional procedures and improving governmental accountability in the context of budget management, legal interpretations, and voter registration.

Document 1: Expedited procedures for congressional consideration of legislation
Recommendations included in the Office of Management and Budget's annual Cuts, Consolidations, and Savings report.
Document 2: Require the Attorney General to report to Congress
Legal interpretations submitted to the President, executive branch agency heads.
Document 3: Require states to permit individuals to register to vote on the date of the election
Amends the National Voter Registration Act of 1993.

topic.name: Public Safety and Security Legislation
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislative measures aimed at enhancing public safety, countering extremism, and addressing accountability in various sectors including education and military.

Document 1: This bill authorizes the Department of Homeland Security (DHS) to provide training at state and major urban area fusion centers for the purpose of administering community awareness briefings and related activities in furtherance of its efforts to counter violent extremism.
DHS shall notify Congress of the number of employees of state, local, tribal, and territorial governments with security clearances sponsored by DHS.
Document 2: Directs the Secretaries of the military departments, to the maximum extent practicable, to make information on civilian credentialing opportunities available to members of the Armed Forces.
Requires such Secretaries to make available to civilian credentialing agencies specified information on the content of military training provided to members.
Document 3: Amends provisions of the Higher Education Act of 1965 (HEA) known as the Jeanne Clery Disclosure of Campus Security Policy and Campus Crime Statistics Act to require institutions of higher education (IHEs) to include in their annual campus security reports.
Requires IHEs to provide new students and employees with a statement that identifies domestic violence, dating violence, sexual assault, and stalking as crimes which will be reported.

topic.name: Health Care Technology and Services
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation aimed at improving health care delivery and outcomes through the use of technology and funding for essential health services.

Document 1: Fostering Independence Through Technology Act of 2009
home monitoring and communications technology
Document 2: Medicare Lung Cancer Early Detection Promotion Act of 2010
Computer-Aided Detection Technology for the early detection of lung cancer
Document 3: HIV Clinical Services Improvement Act
payment under the Medicaid program for services furnished by recipients of Ryan White Part C grants

topic.name: Financial Regulation and Consumer Protection
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative measures aimed at enhancing financial stability and protecting consumers in the banking and mortgage sectors.

Document 1: amends the Dodd-Frank Wall Street Reform and Consumer Protection Act
authorize the Financial Stability Oversight Council (FSOC) to subject a bank holding company to enhanced supervision
Document 2: amends the Gramm-Leach-Bliley Act to exempt from its annual privacy policy notice requirement
directed to study jointly the appropriate capital requirements for mortgage servicing assets
Document 3: Amends the Garn-St Germain Depository Institutions Act of 1982
Requires the lender... to provide the successor homeowner with information about the mortgage loan

topic.name: Public Health and Safety Legislation
topic.description: This cluster of documents discusses various legislative measures aimed at enhancing national preparedness and response to public health emergencies, border security, and maritime fishing regulations.

Document 1: Strengthening National Preparedness and Response for Public Health Emergencies
monitor emerging issues and concerns as they relate to medical and public health preparedness
Document 2: directs the Secretary of Defense to deploy National Guard personnel to assist U.S. Customs and Border Protection
enhance border enforcement preparedness and operational readiness
Document 3: Implements the Agreement on Port State Measures to Prevent, Deter and Eliminate Illegal, Unreported and Unregulated Fishing
authorizes the Secretary of Commerce to designate and publicize ports of entry

topic.name: Housing and Education Policy
topic.description: Legislation aimed at improving housing conditions and educational opportunities for vulnerable populations.

Document 1: reauthorizes the Residential Lead-Based Paint Hazard Reduction Act
grants for lead-based paint hazard reduction in target housing
Document 2: amends the Elementary and Secondary Education Act of 1965
supports the instructional needs of students from low-income families
Document 3: grants made by the Secretary of Veterans Affairs for comprehensive services programs for veterans
provide job training, counseling, placement services, and child care services to expedite the reintegration of such veterans into the labor force

topic.name: Tax Code Amendments
topic.description: Legislative changes to the Internal Revenue Code affecting various tax provisions and regulations.

Document 1: Amends Internal Revenue Code provisions relating to the small issuer exemption from interest expense allocation rules
allow small issuers an election to treat borrowers separately for purposes of issuance limitations
Document 2: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to modify rules and definitions relating to the excise tax on distributions from donor advised funds
Exempts certain scholarship distributions from the tax on distributions from donor advised funds
Document 3: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to reduce to 23% the maximum income tax rate for corporations
set forth rules for the allocation of foreign-related tax credits and deductions

topic.name: Tax Relief Legislation
topic.description: Legislation focused on providing tax relief and incentives for specific sectors, including family-owned businesses, breweries, and renewable energy producers.

Document 1: increase to $1.85 million the maximum reduction amount for alternative valuations of farmland
restore after 2009 the estate tax deduction for family-owned business interests
Document 2: reduce from $18 to $9 the per-barrel tax on beer
allow a further reduction in such tax for brewers who produce not more than 2 million barrels annually
Document 3: allow electric utilities a business-related tax credit for producing and selling specified percentages of renewable electricity
tax credit for renewable electricity generated by wind and solar energy facilities

topic.name: U.S. Economic Legislation
topic.description: This cluster focuses on U.S. legislative acts aimed at economic growth, tax relief, and trade preferences.

Document 1: Amends the Securities Act of 1933 and the Securities Exchange Act of 1934 to define 'emerging growth company'.
Exempts emerging growth companies from the requirement for separate shareholder approval of executive compensation.
Document 2: Amends the Trade Act of 1974 and the African Growth and Opportunity Act to extend duty-free treatment of products from sub-Saharan African countries.
The President must notify Congress before terminating the designation of a beneficiary sub-Saharan African country.
Document 3: Makes permanent the Economic Growth and Tax Relief Reconciliation Act of 2001 for individual taxpayers.
Increases the top marginal estate tax rate from 35% to 40%.

topic.name: Designation of Public Facilities
topic.description: The documents discuss the official naming of various public facilities in honor of individuals who served in the military or community.

Document 1: Designates the facility of the United States Postal Service located at 815 County Road 23 in Tyrone, New York, as the 'Specialist Christopher Scott Post Office Building.'
Document 2: Designates the community-based outpatient clinic of the Department of Veterans Affairs (VA) to be constructed at 3141 Centennial Boulevard, Colorado Springs, Colorado, as the 'PFC Floyd K. Lindstrom Department of Veterans Affairs Clinic.'
Document 3: Designates the facility of the United States Postal Service located at 232 Southwest Johnson Avenue in Burleson, Texas, as the 'Sergeant William Moody Post Office Building.'

topic.name: Legislative Acts on Public Safety and Health
topic.description: This cluster encompasses legislative acts aimed at addressing public health, safety, and regulatory measures related to controlled substances and medical training.

Document 1: Children's Hospital GME Support Reauthorization Act of 2011
extends and reauthorizes appropriations for payments associated with operating approved graduate medical residency training programs.
Document 2: Combating Dangerous Synthetic Stimulants Act of 2011
includes as a Schedule I controlled substance any material, compound, mixture, or preparation which contains 4-methylmethcathinone (Mephedrone) or 3,4-methylenedioxypyrovalerone (MDPV).
Document 3: Citizens Protection Act of 2007
repeals provisions of the federal criminal code that prohibit the possession of a firearm in a school zone.
